events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON. Need assess duct installation residential, unsealed joints, cloth duct tape wrong. Work rough-complete. Need decide fail. Need fill fields. Let's reason.

Scenario: unsealed joints (no mastic/foil tape - cloth duct tape is classic wrong answer). Duct installation. Residential HVAC-R. Code IMC 603 requires duct joints and seams be sealed with approved materials (mastic, foil tape, etc.) and cloth-backed duct tape not approved for sealing. SMACNA and Manual D also require sealed joints to prevent leakage, ensure airflow/efficiency. Work is roughed in. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk maybe medium/high? Leakage reduces efficiency, comfort, energy waste, could cause pressure imbalances, but not immediate safety critical. In residential HVAC, duct leakage can be high risk for combustion appliance backdraft? Not specified. I'd set risk medium or high. Since task says if dangerous or non-compliant decision fail. Risk high maybe because unsealed joints cause significant energy loss and potential IAQ/comfort issues. But not critical. Let's choose high? Need decide. The classic item is non-compliant; risk medium. Let's evaluate: IMC 603.2? Ducts shall be sealed per Section 603.9? Actually IMC 603.9 Seams and Joints: Longitudinal and transverse joints and seams shall be securely fastened and sealed with welds, gaskets, locking mechanisms, lapped sheet metal, or approved tapes or mastic. Cloth duct tape not approved. So fail.

Need produce JSON with fields. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 70%. value not asked 0. sound_source irrelevant empty? Schema says string; can use "". event_time 0, rate 0, order empty list, workable empty list. findings: "Duct joints are unsealed/secured with cloth-backed duct tape instead of approved mastic or foil tape", "Duct leakage violates IMC 603 sealing requirements". actions: "Remove cloth duct tape and seal all longitudinal/transverse joints and seams with approved mastic or UL-181 rated foil tape per manufacturer/SMACNA", "Verify seal with duct leakage test if required". references: "IMC 603", "SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards", "ACCA Manual D". rationale: brief.

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.text: {"decision":{"decision":"fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":70,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.95,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Duct longitudinal/transverse joints are unsealed or sealed with cloth-backed duct tape, which is not an approved duct sealant","Joint sealing does not meet IMC 603 / SMACNA requirements and will leak conditioned air"],"actions":["Remove cloth duct tape from all duct joints and seams","Seal all joints and seams with approved mastic or UL-181 rated foil tape per SMACNA and manufacturer instructions","Perform duct leakage testing if required by code or specification before close-out"],"rationale":"Cloth-backed duct tape is not an approved sealant for duct joints under IMC 603 and SMACNA; unsealed/ improperly sealed joints leak conditioned air, reduce efficiency, and violate code, so the installation fails at rough-complete.","references":["IMC 603","SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards","ACCA Manual D"]}}
